title: Greenhouse emissions per unit of food transported
subtitle: [Greenhouse gases](#dod:ghg) emitted by transporting one tonne of food over a distance of one kilometer. Emissions are measured in kilograms of [carbon dioxide-equivalents](#dod:carbondioxideequivalents).
note: Emission factors can vary depending on factors such as the efficiency of vehicle used; packing/loading density of freight etc.
caption:
Greenhouse emissions per unit of food transported

Greenhouse gases emitted by transporting one tonne of food over a distance of one kilometer. Emissions are measured in kilograms of carbon dioxide-equivalents.

This horizontal bar chart compares greenhouse gas emissions across four modes of food transport: Air, Road, Rail, and Water. The bars are arranged vertically with Air at the top and Water at the bottom, each labeled with a precise value in kilograms of carbon dioxide-equivalents. Air transport shows the highest emission level at 1.13 kg, represented by the longest bar extending far to the right. Road transport follows with a value of 0.4 kg, its bar reaching less than half the length of the Air bar. Rail transport registers 0.06 kg, with a significantly shorter bar that sits near the left edge of the chart. Water transport has the lowest value at 0.02 kg, depicted by the shortest bar, barely extending from the axis. The visual spacing between bars reflects the magnitude of difference: Air’s bar is nearly three times longer than Road’s, while Rail and Water occupy the lower end of the scale with minimal extension. Emission factors can vary depending on factors such as the efficiency of vehicle used; packing/loading density of freight etc. The chart uses uniform pink coloring for all bars, with no additional legends or groupings. Values are explicitly labeled at the end of each bar, allowing direct comparison without reliance on axis ticks. The layout emphasizes the disparity in emissions intensity among transport modes, with Air dominating the upper range and Water anchoring the lower bound.
